steps.title: Identify the time reference
steps.content: The sentence establishes “By 2023”—a specific moment in past time—as the point when her status as a celebrated musician must be described.

steps.title: Apply the past perfect rule
steps.content:
Use past perfect (had + past participle) to show what had already become true by that past date. “Had become” correctly places her achievement as already complete by 2023. The other three fail because they either project forward into the future (“will become,” “will have become”) or use simple present without temporal connection (“becomes”).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
